summary: Fire‑hot second set with Drums>Iko>Women Smarter and epic Other One jam
blurb: Second set erupts with the parade‑style Drums → Iko → Man Smart, Woman Smarter segue into a soaring 'Standing On The Moon' and a monster jam through 'The Other One → Morning Dew → Throwing Stones.' Jerry’s guitar is hot and jazzy, Phil delivers melodic bass lines, and Brent’s keyboards drive the improvisations. The soundboard tape captures crisp instrument separation, making this a standout Mardi Gras run.
review: On February 27, 1990 the Dead delivered a high‑energy Mardi Gras performance at the Oakland Coliseum that still shines in the archives. The first set rolled gently through classics like "Victim Or The Crime" and the sweet "Mississippi Half-Step Uptown Toodeloo," but it was the second set that truly ignited the night. A parade‑style Drums → Iko → Man Smart, Woman Smarter launch propelled the band into a soaring "Standing On The Moon" before launching into an inspired jam corridor—"The Other One → Morning Dew → Throwing Stones"—that showcases Jerry’s scorching, jazzy leads and Phil’s melodic bass excursions. Brent’s keyboards add texture and drive throughout, especially during the Iko/Women Smarter segment, while Mickey’s drumming is described as “spicy” and tight. The show closes on a mellow encore with "And We Bid You Good Night," leaving the audience buzzing. Captured on a clear soundboard tape, the mix offers excellent separation of bass, keys, and drums, making this recording one of the best documents of that Mardi Gras run.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three analyzed recordings carry a positive sentiment, with the primary soundboard source offering detailed praise of multiple standout moments (Drums>Iko>Women Smarter parade, The Other One‑Morning Dew‑Throwing Stones jam) and strong remarks on individual band members. Consistent acclaim across sources justifies a high positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard tape with crisp, well‑balanced mix and strong instrument separation; captures the high‑energy performance in detail.

key_highlights: Parade‑style Drums → Iko → Man Smart, Woman Smarter launch that fuels the second set
Epic jam sequence The Other One → Morning Dew → Throwing Stones
"Mississippi Half-Step Uptown Toodeloo" sweet moment in first set
Crisp soundboard recording with excellent instrument separation

band_performance.Jerry: Hot guitar tone with jazzy leads and strong vocal contributions throughout, especially on Spoonful and Throwing Stones.
band_performance.Phil: Awesome bass output with standout melodic lines during the jam corridors, notably in The Other One–Morning Dew sequence.
band_performance.Bob: Fine vocals and solid axe work on Spoonful and Throwing Stones; rhythm playing noted as steady.
band_performance.Brent: A+ keyboard texture that drives the Iko/Women Smarter jam and adds depth to the improvisations.
band_performance.Mickey: Spicy launch into the Iko sequence and tight drumming throughout the second set.
